_**User**_

Context: I have been trying to integrate the frontend and backend systems. So far, I have been running into issues where the backend does not fully execute. For example, the correct uploaded music sheet does not upload, the fallback is called instead. I am also not able to generate any harmonies.

Task: Integrate frontend and backend application and deploy to a new vercel link. Ignore any previous implementation attempts. Start from scratch. Here is what my colleague said: convert to vercel prompt: To deploy on Vercel, it is better to merge the backend and frontend into one app. The current backend is also in js/ts, so it would be better just to merge the engine as a server-side component of a fullstack Next.js app. In that case, we can just deploy one app without having to deal with routing.

Outline plan for me.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Creating a writing plan**

I’ve loaded the skills and documents and need to mention that I'm using the writing-plans skill as per instructions. The pre-flight checklist requires user to pin documents, so I should remind them about that. Now, I need to call CreatePlan with the outline. Even though there's a conflict about using '#' headings versus the tool spec stating it must be a level 1 heading, I'll prioritize the tool spec. The plan needs to mention integrating the backend engine into a new Next.js app.
**Planning Vercel deployment**

Now we need a new Vercel link with merged architecture, brushing aside previous attempts. This might mean some refactoring is needed: either moving the engine code into the `frontend` as a server-side-only module, or colocating it under a common `engine/` folder. Since Vercel would deploy only the Next.js app, we might face issues with deploying the backend. We could bundle the engine with Next Route Handlers instead. I'll consider creating a new app folder `next-app/` or integrating it into the existing `frontend/`.
**Simplifying the repo structure**

To keep the repository simpler while maintaining the old elements, I’m considering naming the new app something like `app-vercel/` or `frontend-next-merged/`. They want to align with the existing structure, but the user didn’t specify anything particular. The plan can settle on using the default name like "new app `app/`" and reusing the engine through a shared package. We will extract the reusable engine package under `engine/` and import it into the Next route handlers, while also lifting existing frontend pages into the new app.
**Drafting the plan sections**

The plan sections at a high level are:

- Step 0 will focus on alignment and context, like reading the ADRs, plan details, and progress.

- Step 1 will address repo restructuring for a single Next app on Vercel. 

I see two options:  
- Option A is to convert the existing `frontend/` into the fullstack app by incorporating the `backend/engine` into it, like moving the engine code into `frontend/engine` or `frontend/src/engine`.  
- Option B involves creating a `apps/web` and `packages/engine` monorepo, which is more work. I prefer Option A for its simplicity.
**Outlining the integration plan**

The plan should include notes about moving or symlinking resources.

- **Step 2: Engine Integration**  
I’ll move `backend/engine` into `frontend/engine` and update the `tsconfig` and package.json dependencies. Next, I’ll create Next route handlers under `app/api/generate-from-file` and `app/api/to-preview-musicxml`, using code from the previous Express server.

- **Step 3: Frontend**  
I’ll revise client code to call the new internal API routes without needing `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL`, relying on relative paths. It’s also important to fix the upload handler, ensuring correct files are passed and that fallback logic only activates on explicit errors.

- **Step 4: Environment & Configuration**  
I need to set up the environmental variables required by the engine within Vercel.

- **Step 5: Local Testing**  
I'll run tests locally with `npm run dev` for a single app and `npm test`.

- **Step 6: Vercel Deployment**  
For deployment, the project root should point to the new app folder and I’ll configure build/run settings, ensuring file uploads and generation are verified. I'll also add a mermaid diagram to visualize data flow, then proceed with CreatePlan.</details></think>

{
  "name": "merge-backend-into-next-vercel",
  "overview": "Design a clean plan to merge the existing HarmonyForge backend engine and frontend into a single fullstack Next.js app that runs entirely on Vercel, fixing the upload/generation integration issues and deploying to a new Vercel URL.",
  "plan": "# Fullstack Next.js Merge \u0026 Vercel Deployment Plan\n\n## High-level architecture\n\n- **Goal**: Run both the Tactile Sandbox UI and the SATB engine inside a single Next.js App Router app deployed on Vercel, with engine logic compiled into server-only modules and exposed via Next Route Handlers (or Server Actions) instead of a separate Node server.\n- **Strategy**: Reuse the existing &#96;backend/engine&#96; code as a pure TypeScript module, relocate it under the Next app (or as a local package), and replace the current Express/Hono server with &#96;app/api/*&#96; route handlers that call the same engine functions. Point the existing upload + generate flows at these internal routes so there is no cross-origin &#96;NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL&#96; / CORS complexity.\n- **Deployment**: Deploy a single Vercel project with root set to the merged Next app. All compute (upload parsing, harmony generation, theory inspector, etc.) runs in Vercel Functions; long-running or PDF/OMR pieces may be feature-flagged or degraded if they don’t fit the Vercel runtime budget.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart LR\n  user[User Browser]\n  nextApp[NextApp (Vercel)]\n  ui[Playground/Document/Sandbox Pages]\n  apiGen[app/api/generate-from-file]\n  apiPreview[app/api/to-preview-musicxml]\n  engine[Engine Module (SATB, intake)]\n\n  user --\u003e ui\n  ui --\u003e apiPreview\n  ui --\u003e apiGen\n  apiPreview --\u003e engine\n  apiGen --\u003e engine\n  engine --\u003e apiPreview\n  engine --\u003e apiGen\n  apiGen --\u003e ui\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## 1. Align on existing repo and constraints\n\n- **Files to read**: &#96;[docs/plan.md](docs/plan.md)&#96;, &#96;[docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md)&#96;, &#96;[docs/deployment.md](docs/deployment.md)&#96;, &#96;[backend/README.md](backend/README.md)&#96;, &#96;[frontend/README.md](frontend/README.md)&#96;, relevant ADRs under &#96;[docs/adr/](docs/adr/)&#96;.\n- **Decide scope**: Confirm that PDF/OMR (&#96;plan §1.9m&#96;) remains a known risk and that the first merged deployment only needs reliable symbolic formats (MusicXML, MXL, MIDI). Plan to treat PDF as \"best-effort\" or temporarily disable it on Vercel if runtime/tooling is not feasible.\n- **Confirm flow**: Keep the target user path as documented: Upload → Document (preview + config) → Generate → Sandbox, with harmonies generated by the engine and no frontend-only fake fallback.\n\n## 2. Choose folder layout for the merged app\n\n- **Option A (likely simplest)**: Promote the existing &#96;frontend/&#96; Next app to be the single deployable and pull &#96;backend/engine&#96; into it.\n  - Create &#96;frontend/engine/&#96; (or &#96;frontend/src/engine/&#96;) and move the contents of &#96;backend/engine/&#96; there.\n  - Copy any &#96;backend&#96;-specific &#96;package.json&#96; dependencies into &#96;frontend/package.json&#96; (e.g. &#96;@tonejs/midi&#96;, XML parsers, ZIP libs, pdfalto/oemer bindings if still used).\n  - Remove or archive the old &#96;backend/server&#96; entrypoints so they are not used at runtime.\n- **Option B (monorepo package)**: Turn &#96;backend/engine&#96; into a &#96;packages/engine&#96; library and have the Next app depend on it.\n  - Only choose this if you want to keep CLI tooling or separate engine reuse; otherwise Option A is less work.\n- **Plan**: For an immediate Vercel deployment, favor **Option A** and leave the old &#96;backend/&#96; folder for CLI/dev use only (or mark as deprecated in docs).\n\n## 3. Refactor the engine into a server-only module\n\n- **Goal**: Make the SATB solver and file-intake pipeline callable as pure functions from Next server code, with no Express/Hono or direct &#96;listen()&#96; calls.\n- **Steps**:\n  - Identify the current engine entrypoints (e.g. &#96;server.ts&#96;, &#96;fileIntake.ts&#96;, &#96;generateFromFile.ts&#96;, &#96;generateSatb.ts&#96;) under &#96;backend/engine/&#96;.\n  - Extract the core operations into reusable functions, e.g. &#96;generateFromFile(fileBuffer, filename, config)&#96; and &#96;toPreviewMusicXml(fileBuffer, filename)&#96; that return structured results or throw typed errors.\n  - Ensure these functions:\n    - Do not reference &#96;process.cwd()&#96; in ways incompatible with Vercel (replace with &#96;process.env&#96; and explicit paths where needed).\n    - Do not call &#96;process.exit&#96; or any CLI-only behavior.\n    - Handle timeouts and &#96;HF_SOLVER_MAX_MS&#96; purely via parameters/environment (so the Next route can pick a safe default for Vercel).\n  - Keep risky OMR/PDF logic behind a clearly flagged function (e.g. &#96;generateFromPdf&#96;) so it can be disabled or guarded for Vercel.\n\n## 4. Implement Next.js Route Handlers for engine endpoints\n\n- **Goal**: Replace the separate backend HTTP server with internal Next APIs.\n- **Files to add/modify (under &#96;frontend/&#96;)**:\n  - &#96;app/api/generate-from-file/route.ts&#96;\n    - Accept &#96;POST&#96; multipart/form-data with the uploaded file and optional JSON &#96;config&#96; field.\n    - Read the file into a Buffer/Uint8Array, pass to &#96;generateFromFile&#96;, and return &#96;200&#96; with MusicXML string (or structured JSON) on success.\n    - Map engine errors and timeouts to appropriate HTTP status codes (&#96;422&#96; for invalid score, &#96;504&#96;/&#96;429&#96; for budget exceeded, etc.).\n  - &#96;app/api/to-preview-musicxml/route.ts&#96;\n    - Accept &#96;POST&#96; multipart or binary body for non-XML inputs.\n    - Call &#96;toPreviewMusicXml&#96; and return preview MusicXML used by Playground/Document.\n  - Any additional routes currently used (&#96;/api/validate-from-file&#96;, &#96;/api/theory-inspector&#96;, etc.), reimplemented as Next route handlers that call into the same engine/LLM client modules.\n- **Server-only enforcement**:\n  - Place engine imports in &#96;app/api/*&#96; or &#96;src/server/*&#96; files that run only on the server.\n  - Use &#96;\"use server\"&#96; plus clear layering (&#96;lib/engine/*&#96;) to avoid bundling heavy deps into the client.\n\n## 5. Rewire the frontend upload and generate flows to the new APIs\n\n- **Goal**: Ensure the UI calls the internal Next APIs instead of a separate backend URL, and that fallbacks don’t silently hide genuine engine failures.\n- **Steps**:\n  - Search in &#96;frontend/src&#96; for any references to &#96;NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL&#96;, &#96;CORS_ORIGIN&#96;, or hard-coded engine URLs.\n  - Update the upload dropzones, Document page, and Sandbox actions to call relative paths like &#96;/api/to-preview-musicxml&#96; and &#96;/api/generate-from-file&#96;.\n  - Tighten fallback logic:\n    - Only show a client-side fallback preview when the engine explicitly returns a non-recoverable error (e.g. unsupported format), not on generic network failures.\n    - Log or surface a clear error message when the engine route fails, so you can see when harmonies are not being generated instead of silently calling old stub logic.\n  - Verify that the correct uploaded score is passed into the engine (no stale file in Zustand, no mismatch between Playground and Document when using engine preview XML).\n\n## 6. Environment and configuration for Vercel\n\n- **Goal**: Centralize all environment variables in the Next app so Vercel configuration is straightforward.\n- **Steps**:\n  - Collect engine-related env vars (e.g. &#96;HF_SOLVER_MAX_MS&#96;, any OMR paths, study conditions) and define them in &#96;frontend/.env.example&#96; with clear comments about Vercel usage.\n  - For LLM-related env (Theory Inspector), ensure server-side &#96;OPENAI_API_KEY&#96; or similar is **not** exposed via &#96;NEXT_PUBLIC_*&#96;; use server env only.\n  - Decide on safe defaults for Vercel:\n    - Bounded solver max time per request.\n    - Possibly disable or heavily guard PDF/OMR on Vercel, with copy that explains the limitation.\n  - Update &#96;[docs/deployment.md](docs/deployment.md)&#96; (or add a new section) describing the \"single Next app on Vercel\" path alongside (or replacing) the old \"separate backend host\" guidance.\n\n## 7. Local end-to-end verification of the merged app\n\n- **Goal**: Prove that the merged Next app behaves correctly locally before touching Vercel.\n- **Steps**:\n  - From the Next app root (e.g. &#96;frontend/&#96;), run &#96;npm install&#96; (if needed) and &#96;npm run dev&#96;.\n  - Manually test:\n    - Upload a known-good MusicXML file, verify Document preview uses the engine when appropriate.\n    - Click Generate and confirm harmonies are produced and visible in Sandbox.\n    - Try an unsupported or malformed file and ensure errors are clear rather than falling back silently.\n  - Run existing tests (&#96;make test&#96;, &#96;npm test&#96; in frontend) and adjust any paths/imports broken by moving the engine.\n\n## 8. Vercel deployment: new project and configuration\n\n- **Goal**: Deploy the merged app to a **new** Vercel project/URL, keeping the old deployment intact until the new one is verified.\n- **Steps**:\n  - In Vercel, create a new project pointing at this Git repo, with **root directory** set to the merged Next app folder (likely &#96;frontend/&#96;).\n  - Set the build command (e.g. &#96;npm run build&#96;) and output directory defaults (Next.js standard).\n  - Configure all required environment variables in Vercel’s dashboard for the new project, mirroring the &#96;.env.example&#96; you prepared.\n  - Trigger an initial deployment and wait for it to complete.\n\n## 9. Post-deploy smoke tests and cutover\n\n- **Goal**: Validate the new URL end-to-end and then adopt it as the canonical HarmonyForge link.\n- **Steps**:\n  - On the new Vercel URL, run the same manual tests as in §7: upload → preview → generate → sandbox; plus basic Theory Inspector flows if enabled.\n  - Confirm that the correct (latest) uploaded sheet is used and that harmonies are actually being generated (no fallback-only behavior).\n  - If everything passes, update any internal docs (and, if desired, &#96;docs/deployment.md&#96;) to point to the new Vercel URL as the primary deployment.\n\n## Todos\n\n- **merge-layout**: Decide between moving &#96;backend/engine&#96; into &#96;frontend/engine&#96; (Option A) or creating a shared &#96;packages/engine&#96; module, and update docs to reflect the new single-app architecture.\n- **engine-module**: Refactor engine entrypoints into pure functions (&#96;generateFromFile&#96;, &#96;toPreviewMusicXml&#96;, etc.) that can be imported by Next Route Handlers.\n- **next-api-routes**: Implement &#96;app/api/generate-from-file&#96; and &#96;app/api/to-preview-musicxml&#96; using the engine module, with clear error mapping and timeouts suitable for Vercel.\n- **frontend-rewire**: Point all frontend upload/generate flows at the new internal &#96;/api/*&#96; routes and remove reliance on &#96;NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL&#96;/CORS-based backend calls.\n- **env-and-deploy-docs**: Consolidate environment variables in the Next app and update deployment documentation for the single-app Vercel path.\n- **vercel-new-project**: Create and configure a new Vercel project for the merged app, then run smoke tests and adopt the new URL once verified.\n",

**Evaluating integration strategy**

It could be safer to bring the engine code directly into the frontend to avoid any complications.
**Planning engine implementation**

I’ll be copying the engine into `frontend/src/server/engine` while adjusting the imports to be relative and changing the package.json dependencies, keeping the backend/engine unchanged. I need to ensure that the Node environment uses ESM instead of CJS. Since the backend is a module type, we’ll make use of .js extensions for imports. For the frontend in TypeScript, I can import without .js. I plan to copy files like types.ts and solver.ts, and create an index file for easier exports. It sounds like a lot of work, but it should streamline things!
**Analyzing Vercel build options**

I'm considering whether importing the backend engine into a new wrapper and setting Vercel's build root to the repo root would be better. Instead, I think copying the engine while keeping the backend as a git subdirectory could work. I'll go for that and copy essential files—about 20 or so—using ApplyPatch. It's going to be time-consuming, but I can manage!
**Deciding on file copying approach**

I’m considering whether to use Node's `fs`, but I’ll need to edit with ApplyPatch instead. I will copy only what's necessary for the fileIntake pipeline and generateSATB functions. I need to check dependencies since server.ts relies on several components from solver and types, including functions like parseMusicXML and validateSATB. I plan to copy those and their transitive dependencies too, like parsers and chordParser. Starting with `frontend/src/server/engine/`, I’ll gather everything from the backend while adjusting imports and exports. Let’s begin with `types.ts`!</details></think>

_**Agent (premium)**_

At this point the engine no longer needs to “run a backend server” to work: it’s callable as pure server-side functions (`hfEngineApi.ts`) and is already exposed through Next route handlers under `frontend/src/app/api/*`. Next I’ll switch the remaining frontend callers from `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` to relative `/api/...` so uploads and generation always hit the in-app engine (eliminating the fallback/incorrect-file issues caused by cross-origin routing).

Failed to find context:
# Deployment guide

> **HarmonyForge is two services:** a **Next.js** app (`frontend/`) and a **Node/Express** engine (`backend/`).  
> **Vercel** hosts the frontend well; the engine must run on a **separate** host that keeps a long-lived Node process (Railway, Render, Fly.io, Cloud Run, etc.).

Pair this with **[progress.md](progress.md)** (current blockers) and **[plan.md](plan.md)** (checklist).

---

## Order of operations

```text
1. Deploy backend  →  get https://your-api.example.com
2. Create Vercel project (root directory = frontend)
3. Set Vercel env   →  NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL = that API URL
4. Deploy Vercel    →  get https://your-app.vercel.app
5. Set backend env  →  CORS_ORIGIN = exact Vercel origin (https://…)
6. Restart backend  →  browser smoke test (upload → generate → sandbox)
```

---

## 1. Backend (engine)

| Setting | Typical value |
|---------|----------------|
| **Project root** | `backend` (on hosts that support monorepo subfolders) |
| **Install** | `npm ci` or `npm install` |
| **Build** | `npm run build:engine` (outputs `engine/dist/`) |
| **Start** | `node engine/dist/server.js` (use a **production** start, not `tsx watch`) |
| **Port** | Engine defaults to **8000**; many platforms set **`PORT`** — map the platform port to the process or extend the server to read `process.env.PORT` if needed. |

**Environment**

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| **`CORS_ORIGIN`** | Must match your **frontend origin** exactly (scheme + host, no path), e.g. `https://my-app.vercel.app`. Default in code is `http://localhost:3000`. |
| **`PORT`** | Listen port (many hosts inject this). Default **8000** in code. |

**Generation limits (long scores):** **`HF_MAX_CHORD_SLOTS`** (default 128) widens the chord grid so inference stays tractable; **`HF_SOLVER_MAX_NODES`** / **`HF_SOLVER_MAX_MS`** cap SATB backtracking. See [backend/.env.example](../backend/.env.example).

Optional PDF/OMR: **`PDFALTO_BIN`**, **`POPPLER_PDFTOPPM`**, **`OEMER_BIN`** — see [plan.md](plan.md) section **1.9m**. Reference container for Python + oemer: [backend/docker/oemer-omr.Dockerfile](../backend/docker/oemer-omr.Dockerfile).

**PDF / MXL / MIDI preview failures on Upload:** the Playground calls the engine’s **`POST /api/to-preview-musicxml`**. If the response is an error, check engine logs and host tooling; contributors should read **[progress.md — Multi-format intake & PDF](progress.md#multi-format-pdf-intake)** (oemer checkpoints, Python 3.10–12, Poppler, pdfalto).

---

## 2. Frontend (Vercel)

1. **Import** the GitHub repo into Vercel.
2. **Root Directory:** `frontend` (required).
3. **Framework:** Next.js (auto).

**Environment variables (Vercel → Settings → Environment Variables)**

| Variable | Where it runs | Notes |
|----------|----------------|--------|
| **`NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL`** | Browser bundle | **Public.** Base URL of the engine, e.g. `https://your-api.example.com`. Changing it requires a **new deployment** (build-time inlining). |
| **`NEXT_PUBLIC_GENERATE_TIMEOUT_MS`** | Browser | Optional. Abort **`generate-from-file`** after this many ms (default **180000**). Set high enough for your typical inputs; PDFs can spend most time in OMR. |
| **`OPENAI_API_KEY`** | Server (API routes) | **Secret.** Theory Inspector; omit for offline fallbacks where supported. |
| **`OPENAI_BASE_URL`** / **`OPENAI_URL`** | Server | Optional compatible API base. |
| **`OPENAI_MODEL`** | Server | Optional. |
| M5 **`NEXT_PUBLIC_HF_*`** | Browser | Study toggles — see [plan.md](plan.md) M5 section. |

> **Never** put API keys in **`NEXT_PUBLIC_*`** variables — they are exposed in the client bundle.

Template for local dev: **[../frontend/.env.example](../frontend/.env.example)**.

---

## 3. CORS and preview deployments

The engine sends a **single** `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header from **`CORS_ORIGIN`**.

| Scenario | What to do |
|----------|------------|
| **Production** | Set **`CORS_ORIGIN`** to your production Vercel URL (or custom domain). |
| **Vercel Preview** | Each preview has a **different** `*.vercel.app` URL. One fixed **`CORS_ORIGIN`** will **not** allow all previews unless you run a **staging** API with that preview origin, or **change the server** to allow multiple origins (not implemented in-repo today). |

---

## 4. Safety checklist

- [ ] **`.env.local`** and **`.env`** under `frontend/` are **gitignored**; secrets live in **Vercel** / backend dashboard only.
- [ ] **`NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL`** points to **HTTPS** in production.
- [ ] **`CORS_ORIGIN`** matches the **exact** frontend URL users open in the browser.
- [ ] Backend process is **started with `node`** on compiled `dist`, not dev watch mode.

---

# Deployment guide

> **HarmonyForge is one fullstack app:** a **Next.js** app (`frontend/`) with the SATB engine running **server-side** via **Next Route Handlers** (`/api/*`).\n+> **Vercel** can host the UI and engine together (no separate backend host, no CORS routing).\n+\n+Pair this with **[progress.md](progress.md)** (current blockers) and **[plan.md](plan.md)** (checklist).\n+\n+---\n+\n+## Order of operations\n+\n+```text\n+1. Create a new Vercel project (root directory = frontend)\n+2. Set Vercel env (OPENAI_* optional; HF_* solver limits optional)\n+3. Deploy Vercel → get https://your-app.vercel.app\n+4. Browser smoke test (upload → generate → sandbox)\n+```\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 1. Fullstack app (Vercel)\n+\n+1. **Import** the GitHub repo into Vercel.\n+2. **Root Directory:** `frontend` (required).\n+3. **Framework:** Next.js (auto).\n+\n+**Environment variables (Vercel → Settings → Environment Variables)**\n+\n+| Variable | Where it runs | Notes |\n+|----------|----------------|--------|\n+| **`NEXT_PUBLIC_GENERATE_TIMEOUT_MS`** | Browser | Optional. Abort **`generate-from-file`** after this many ms (default **180000**). Set high enough for your typical inputs; PDFs can spend most time in OMR. |\n+| **`OPENAI_API_KEY`** | Server (API routes) | **Secret.** Theory Inspector; omit for offline fallbacks where supported. |\n+| **`OPENAI_BASE_URL`** / **`OPENAI_URL`** | Server | Optional compatible API base. |\n+| **`OPENAI_MODEL`** | Server | Optional. |\n+| **`HF_SOLVER_MAX_MS`** | Server | Optional. Wall-clock SATB limit for file routes. **0 = unlimited**; if unset, file routes default to ~108s. |\n+| **`HF_SOLVER_MAX_NODES`** | Server | Optional. SATB node budget. |\n+| **`HF_MAX_CHORD_SLOTS`** | Server | Optional. Caps chord-grid density for long melodies. |\n+| M5 **`NEXT_PUBLIC_HF_*`** | Browser | Study toggles — see [plan.md](plan.md) M5 section. |\n+\n+> **Never** put API keys in **`NEXT_PUBLIC_*`** variables — they are exposed in the client bundle.\n+\n+Template for local dev: **[../frontend/.env.example](../frontend/.env.example)**.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 2. Notes on PDF/OMR\n+\n+PDF preview/generation still depends on external tooling (`pdfalto`, Poppler `pdftoppm`, and Python `oemer`). On Vercel this is typically **not** available; prefer **MusicXML / MXL / MIDI** for production deploys unless you’ve explicitly built a custom runtime.\n+\n+More details and current status live in **[progress.md — Multi-format intake & PDF](progress.md#multi-format-pdf-intake)** and **[plan.md](plan.md)** section **1.9m**.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 3. Safety checklist\n+\n+- [ ] **`.env.local`** and **`.env`** under `frontend/` are **gitignored**; secrets live in **Vercel** only.\n+- [ ] No secrets are placed in `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` variables.\n+- [ ] Solver budgets are tuned (`HF_SOLVER_MAX_MS`, `HF_SOLVER_MAX_NODES`) so requests fail with clear 4xx errors instead of timing out.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## Related docs\n+\n+| Doc | Why |\n+|-----|-----|\n+| [README.md](../README.md) | Local run, Makefile, folder map |\n+| [../frontend/README.md](../frontend/README.md) | Routes, `src/` layout, patch-package |\n+| [../backend/README.md](../backend/README.md) | Legacy engine layout (kept for CLI/tests) |\n*** End Patch"} } but ApplyPatch expects freeform not json. We'll do freeform.

## What I implemented (fullstack merge)

### Engine now runs inside the Next.js app (no separate backend required)
- **Added a server-only engine module** under `frontend/src/server/engine/` (solver, chord inference, MusicXML/MXL/MIDI intake, MusicXML output).
- **Added a thin server API wrapper**: `frontend/src/server/engine/hfEngineApi.ts`.

### New internal API routes (Next Route Handlers)
These replace the old Express backend routes and are called via relative URLs:
- `frontend/src/app/api/to-preview-musicxml/route.ts`
- `frontend/src/app/api/generate-from-file/route.ts`
- `frontend/src/app/api/validate-from-file/route.ts`
- `frontend/src/app/api/validate-satb-trace/route.ts`
- `frontend/src/app/api/export-chord-chart/route.ts`

### Frontend rewired to call internal `/api/*` (no `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL`)
Removed all `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` / `NEXT_PUBLIC_ENGINE_URL` usage and any hardcoded `http://localhost:8000` calls:
- Updated upload preview + generate flows in:
  - `frontend/src/app/page.tsx`
  - `frontend/src/app/document/page.tsx`
  - `frontend/src/components/organisms/HomeViewOnboarding.tsx`
- Updated export validation + chord chart calls in:
  - `frontend/src/components/molecules/ExportOptionsPane.tsx`
  - `frontend/src/app/sandbox/page.tsx`
- Updated hint text:
  - `frontend/src/lib/ui/intakeErrorHints.ts`

### Dependencies + env/docs updated for the new architecture
- **Frontend deps added** in `frontend/package.json`: `@tonejs/midi`, `adm-zip`, `fast-xml-parser`, `musicxml-interfaces`, `@types/adm-zip`.
- **Env template updated**: `frontend/.env.example` (removed `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL`; added `HF_SOLVER_*` guidance).
- **Deployment doc updated**: `docs/deployment.md` now describes **single Vercel fullstack deploy**.

## Remaining todo: “deploy to a new Vercel link”
I attempted to run installs/tests and Vercel deployment tooling, but **process execution is currently blocked in this environment** (even simple shell commands aren’t completing, and Vercel MCP tool invocation failed). Because of that, I can’t generate the new Vercel URL from here.

### What you can do to deploy (will produce the new Vercel URL)
- **Create a new Vercel project** pointing at this repo with **Root Directory = `frontend`**
- Set env vars (optional):
  - `OPENAI_API_KEY` (if you want Theory Inspector online mode)
  - `HF_SOLVER_MAX_MS`, `HF_SOLVER_MAX_NODES`, `HF_MAX_CHORD_SLOTS` (optional tuning)
- Deploy; the app will use internal `/api/*` routes automatically (no CORS / no separate backend).

## Todo status
- **Completed**: `merge-layout`, `engine-module`, `next-api-routes`, `frontend-rewire`, `env-and-deploy-docs`
- **Not completed (blocked here)**: `vercel-new-project` (deployment + new live URL)

Superman: if you want, paste the Vercel project name you created (or a screenshot of the Vercel “Import Project” settings), and I’ll tell you the exact env var set + the quickest smoke test to confirm upload → preview → generate → sandbox on the new URL.

_**Agent (premium)**_

I instrumented the runtime paths and set up hypothesis-based logging for this debug session.  
I also cleared prior logs (file did not exist yet, so the next run will start clean).

### Hypotheses under test

- **H1 — Upload classification bug:** `needsEnginePreviewForExtension()` is misclassifying your uploaded file extension, causing a non-engine parse path and fallback behavior.
- **H2 — Preview API intake failure:** `/api/to-preview-musicxml` is being called, but engine intake fails (unsupported format/sniff mismatch/PDF tooling path), so the frontend catches the error and falls back.
- **H3 — Generate request contract issue:** `/api/generate-from-file` is receiving malformed payload (missing file/config parse issue) or returns structured failure before solver runs.
- **H4 — Solver budget/complexity latency:** generation is reaching SATB solver budget (or near-budget) due to input complexity/chord slot count, causing very long latency and eventual failure.
- **H5 — Latency concentrated before/after solver:** total latency is disproportionately in intake/normalization vs SATB solve, which changes what we should optimize first.

### Instrumentation added (all tagged to session `b18196`)
- Client upload entry + client preview failure catch
- `/api/to-preview-musicxml` route receive/fail/success with elapsed ms
- `/api/generate-from-file` route receive/fail/success with elapsed ms
- Engine generate internals: solver success and solver budget exceeded (elapsed/chord slots)

<reproduction_steps>
1. Restart the app/runtime you are using so the new instrumentation is active.
2. Upload the same music sheet that currently falls back, then proceed to Document.
3. Attempt Generate Harmonies on the same score and wait until it succeeds or fails.
4. Press Proceed/Mark as fixed when done.
</reproduction_steps>

_**Agent (premium)**_

Implemented and deployed a real fix for both issues.

### What I changed

- **Stopped fallback parsing behavior** by normalizing uploads through the engine preview path first.
  - `needsEnginePreviewForExtension()` now routes uploads through `/api/to-preview-musicxml` so parsing is consistent.
- **Cut generation latency** by reusing the already-normalized preview XML for generation.
  - On `/document`, Generate now sends `storePreviewXml` (when available) instead of reprocessing the original uploaded file.
  - This avoids expensive re-intake/OMR loops and reduces parsing mismatches.
- **Removed temporary debug instrumentation** from runtime code.
- **Redeployed to Vercel production**.

### Live link (updated fix)

- `https://frontend-sjp10-9620s-projects.vercel.app`

### Important expectation

- This should now be much more reliable for **MusicXML / MXL / MIDI / mislabeled symbolic files**.
- **PDF remains best-effort** (OMR/tooling constraint), but the new flow avoids redundant heavy processing when preview already succeeded.

If you want, I can next add a hard “no-fallback” user-facing diagnostic panel that shows exactly why a file failed (format/solver/timeout) instead of generic failure text.

This page requires Vercel authentication. Here are your options:

Option 1: vercel curl (Recommended if Vercel CLI installed)
If the Vercel CLI is installed and authenticated, use the &#96;vercel curl&#96; command. Docs at: https://vercel.com/docs/cli/curl

Option 2: Vercel MCP Server (Recommended if Vercel CLI not installed)
If the Vercel CLI is not installed, the easiest way to get a token is using the get_access_to_vercel_url or web_fetch_vercel_url functions of the Vercel MCP server at https://mcp.vercel.com (Docs at https://vercel.com/docs/mcp/vercel-mcp)
  
Option 3: Bypass token (Manual)
This URL documents how to use the bypass token: https://vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation

function showCheckIconWhenVercelJwtIsSet(){fetch('/.well-known/vercel-user-meta').then((response)=&gt;{if(response.status===200){document.getElementById('spinner').classList.add('disappear')
document.getElementById('check').classList.add('appear')

_**Agent (premium)**_

Those messages are now addressed end-to-end.

- Root cause was real: the deployed Vercel project had no `OPENAI_API_KEY`, so the UI correctly showed offline tutor warnings.
- I added production env vars on Vercel and redeployed:
  - `OPENAI_API_KEY`
  - `OPENAI_BASE_URL=https://openai.gateway.salt-lab.org/v1`
- I verified the live API reports tutor enabled: `/api/theory-inspector` now returns `{"status":"ok","hasApiKey":true}`.
- I also updated the fallback copy so it no longer implies only local setup:
  - `frontend/src/components/organisms/TheoryInspectorPanel.tsx`
  - `frontend/src/app/api/theory-inspector/route.ts`
  - New wording mentions both local `.env.local` and Vercel Project Settings.

Use your stable URL: [https://frontend-sjp10-9620s-projects.vercel.app](https://frontend-sjp10-9620s-projects.vercel.app)

If you still see the old warnings, do a hard refresh once (cached JS from the previous deploy can keep old text temporarily).

---

_**User**_

how do i do hard refresh


---

_**Agent (premium)**_

- **Mac Chrome / Edge / Brave:** press `Cmd + Shift + R`
- **Mac Safari:** press `Cmd + Option + R`
- **Firefox (Mac):** press `Cmd + Shift + R`

If it still looks stale after that, open a new Incognito/Private window and load  
[https://frontend-sjp10-9620s-projects.vercel.app](https://frontend-sjp10-9620s-projects.vercel.app) to bypass most cached assets.

```

  ▲ vercel link command [options]

  Link a local directory to a Vercel Project.                                   

  Commands:

  add    Add additional Vercel Projects to an existing repository link. 
         Requires an existing repo.json (created by &#96;link --repo&#96;).     


  Options:

  -p,  --project &lt;NAME_OR_ID&gt;    Project name or ID to link to (required for      
                                 non-interactive)                                 
  -r,  --repo                    Link multiple projects based on Git repository   
                                 (alpha)                                          
       --team &lt;TEAM_ID_OR_SLUG&gt;  Scope: team ID or slug (use with --project for   
                                 non-interactive)                                 
  -y,  --yes                     Skip questions when setting up new project using 
                                 default scope and settings                       


  Global Options:

       --cwd &lt;DIR&gt;            Sets the current working directory for a single   
                              run of a command                                  
  -d,  --debug                Debug mode (default off)                          
  -Q,  --global-config &lt;DIR&gt;  Path to the global &#96;.vercel&#96; directory            
  -h,  --help                 Output usage information                          
  -A,  --local-config &lt;FILE&gt;  Path to the local &#96;vercel.json&#96; file              
       --no-color             No color mode (default off)                       
       --non-interactive      Run without interactive prompts; when an agent is 
                              detected this is the default                      
  -S,  --scope                Set a custom scope                                
  -t,  --token &lt;TOKEN&gt;        Login token                                       
  -v,  --version              Output the version number                         


  Examples:

  - Link current directory to a Vercel Project

    $ vercel link

  - Link current directory with default options and skip questions

    $ vercel link --yes

  - Non-interactive: link to an existing project (CI/agents)

    $ vercel link --yes --team &lt;team-id&gt; --project &lt;project-name-or-id&gt;

  - Link a specific directory to a Vercel Project

    $ vercel link --cwd /path/to/project

  - Link to the current Git repository, allowing for multiple Vercel Projects to be linked simultaneously (useful for monorepos)

    $ vercel link --repo

  - Add additional projects to an existing repository link

    $ vercel link add


```
